title: What I think about Bigfoot - I believe

transcript.text: Hey, what's up? This video is what I think about Bigfoot, because somebody just asked on one of my videos to say what I think about Bigfoot.
And I think it's a skeptical person, and they don't believe in Bigfoot.

Now, I originally wanted to wait until I did some research about it before I answered this video, but it's just something that doesn't interest me, so I'll just go ahead and do the video, what I think about Bigfoot.
To answer simply, I do believe in Bigfoot.
I believe in Bigfoot. It's pretty simple why I believe in Bigfoot. It's because people believe in Bigfoot.
And not just one person, but lots of people. It's kind of like UFOs. If enough people say something, then of course it's true.
Now you might say, oh yeah, people are really creative. Yeah, people are creative, but not saying I saw this and I saw that and everybody decided that they believe it.
Tinzen Norgay, the first guy to climb Mount Everest with Edmund Hillary, saw one.
And I sailed across Lake Titicaca with this Chinese-Canadian guy who spent like four months backpacking in Bhutan.
Because of just magical circumstances, he was able to do that.
They all thought he was Bhutanese and he dressed up in Bhutanese clothing and he was using money, like shells as money.
It was when they were still doing that.
And he says they all believe in orbs.
And he actually saw a bunch of orbs fly in front of his head, in front of his face and split apart and fly off.
And this is a scientist, some kind of geologist.
And he said they all believe in Bigfoot and he saw a big Bigfoot skin pinned up against a wall in a restaurant in Bhutan.
I listened to an interview in Coast to Coast by this guy who was a Bigfoot studier.
And it seemed, you know, at the end of the interview that it was like, oh yeah, you have to believe in Bigfoot.
Because like they had like all sorts of like recordings of their voice, you know, scat of it.
And I guess the video, that famous video of the Bigfoot lady who turns around and looks at the guy in the riverbed and walks off.
Like people say that the shoulders are way too wide and they see the arms, you know, moving as the person walks.
And just by looking where the hinges of their arms are on their shoulders, you can tell that the shoulders are way too wide for a human.
And so there's no way that, and the way that it was walking is there's no way you could, you know, so accurately walk like that.
You know, creating some kind of like a robot or like suit or whatever to make those fake arms hit, you know, hinge like that.
So they were saying it was real.
Now, how could you possibly believe in something that is so elusive?
Well, we have the tendency to believe that we've got it all figured out.
And the main reason I used to say that we don't is because we've always felt like we've had it all figured out, you know.
We always feel like we have it all figured out.
And then, whoops, we don't have it all figured out.
And then we forget that before we thought we had it all figured out, before we realized we didn't have it all figured out, you know.
And it happens over and over again.
Then we had it all figured out that this earth was the beginning of the, we had it all figured out that the earth was flat.
Then we had it all figured out that the earth was the center of the solar system.
Then we had it all figured out that we couldn't fly.
Then we all figured out that we couldn't break the sound barrier without a plane, you know, busting apart.
Then we had it all figured out that, you know, like you couldn't run faster than a four-minute mile.
Then we all had it figured out that, you know, there was no plate tectonics.
Then we had it all, now we have it all figured out that the earth isn't growing.
And all these plate tectonics are spreading apart everywhere, but, you know, the earth isn't growing.
And, you know, and look, we have it all figured out that there's no aliens here, even though it's fucking obvious.
So, I mean, how many times are we going to have to have it all figured out before we realize we don't have it all figured out?
And so we think we, you know, we haven't even seen a giant squid.
Like we got a first photo of a tent, part of a tentacle of a giant squid, just like last year.
The only reason we know they exist is because we know that they're squid and that you see whales with these gigantic cuts in their bodies.
And just judging by the size of the cuts, you have to know that they come from a squid that's that big.
And that's the only reason we know.
So there's all kinds of animals, you know, and there's all kinds of fish we find every year.
You know, we just found the three-foot-tall pygmy bones just a couple years ago, you know.
I mean, so Bigfoot's just another animal that we haven't found yet.
Now, they can be elusive.
I mean, there's all kinds of ways.
Maybe they're intelligent and they just know where the buttons are to get in their cave, you know.
Or maybe they're intelligent, like they're just another alien.
But they don't want to talk to us because they'd scare us because they look scary because they're hairy and big.
So they just fly here from the outer space and they walk around and they like hanging out in the woods because there's lots of woods where the planet where they come from or something like that.
You might say, well, why would an intelligent, you know, person have so much hair on their body like that?
Well, I mean, hair doesn't have to be necessarily that inconvenient to have.
Maybe they just have hair instead of clothes.
I don't know.
So anyways, I'm just going to go ahead and say that I believe in Bigfoot, although I don't ever think about him.
I'm going to believe him because so many people say they've seen him and I don't want to call those people liars because I don't do that.
But I don't think about him because, you know, I'm not into Bigfoot.
I'm more into like, you know, Venetians and Pleiadians and aliens that look like us, you know.
So but anyways, yeah, I basically am going to say that I believe in Bigfoot, but I never think about it, you know, and it's so it's kind of like it's kind of like that.
I don't live with Bigfoot in my mind all the time, so it's kind of like you could call me a not a skeptic, but I don't know.
Anyways, it doesn't really matter to me.
So there you go.
But I don't know anything about Bigfoot, so I mean, if somebody were to show me good evidence, they don't exist.
Well, maybe I believe that, too, but it's just not that big of a deal for me.
